content.problemStatement:
In ChefLand, there is a mountain range consisting of NNN hills (numbered 111 through NNN) in a straight line. Let's denote the height of the iii-th hill from the left by hih_ihi​.

Ada is working on the water supply system of ChefLand. On some of the hills, she wants to place water reservoirs; then, for each reservoir, she will decide in which direction the water should flow from it — either to the left or to the right (water may not flow in both directions from the same reservoir). From a reservoir on a hill with height hhh, water flows in the chosen direction until it reaches the first hill that is strictly higher than hhh; all hills before this hill (including the hill containing the reservoir) are therefore supplied with water.

For example, suppose we have hills with heights [7,2,3,5,8][7, 2, 3, 5, 8][7,2,3,5,8]. If we place a reservoir on the hill with height 555, and pump water from it to the left, then the hills with heights 222, 333 and 555 are supplied with water.

Help Ada find the minimum numer of reservoirs needed to provide water to all the hills if she chooses the directions optimally.
constraints: hi ≠ hj for any valid i ≠ j
the sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 5 * 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line of the input contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows. The first line of each test case contains a single integer N. The second line contains N space-separated integers h1, h2, …, hN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer — the minimum required number of reservoirs.

sampleTestCases.explanation: We can place reservoirs on the second and third hill, pumping water to the left and right respectively.
We can place a reservoir on the last hill and pump water to the left to cover all hills.
We can place a reservoir on the first hill and pump water to the right to cover all hills.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use a greedy algorithm. We start by initializing two pointers, one at the beginning and one at the end of the array. We then move the pointers towards each other, placing reservoirs at the highest hills that have not been covered yet. We keep track of the minimum number of reservoirs needed to cover all hills.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum number of hills in a test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — for storing the heights of the hills
